task_context.actionable_question: What specific risk signals are active for me right now?
task_context.ai_prompt_hint: Treat this as a checklist: list triggered flags first, then explain each using flag_value vs threshold, severity, flag_description, and recommended_action. Keep non-triggered flags brief.

explanation.raw_text:
Summary: You have a few important risk signals that indicate areas where you can improve your academic performance. Addressing these can help you get back on track and succeed in your studies.

Insights: Repeated Attempts: You have previously attempted this course, which may suggest you are facing some challenges. It's important to understand the context of these attempts to provide the right support. | Low Punctuality: Your submission punctuality is below the expected threshold, which can impact your overall performance. Setting reminders and submitting drafts earlier can help you improve in this area. | Negative Trend in Scores: Your scores are trending downward, which is a concern. It's essential to review recent feedback and compare it with your earlier work to identify areas for improvement.

Educational implications: Addressing these risk signals can help you improve your academic performance and reduce the likelihood of further challenges.

Recommendations: high - Check prior attempt context and confirm whether you need a catch-up plan. - Understanding your previous attempts can help tailor support to your needs. | high - Use deadline reminders and submit drafts earlier. - Improving punctuality can enhance your overall performance and reduce stress. | high - Compare recent feedback with earlier stronger work and intervene before the next assessment. - Identifying areas of decline can help you focus your efforts effectively.

required_core_outputs.description: Explain each triggered flag using its value, threshold, severity, description, and recommended action when available.

evaluation_constraints.description: If no flags are triggered, state that explicitly.
Do not invent risk signals that are not present in returned flags.

evaluation_requirements.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.
